name: chart/treemap
description: Squarified treemap for visualizing part-to-whole hierarchical data.

meta.name: Treemap
meta.description: Squarified treemap for visualizing part-to-whole hierarchical data.

props.name: data
props.description: The hierarchical data to render as nested rectangles.
props.type: { id: string; label: string; value: number }[]

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

props.name: plain
props.description: Marks only — no axes, grid lines, or legend. For micro/inline charts.

examples.title: Basic treemap
examples.code:
import { Treemap } from '@cascivo/charts'

const data = [
  {id:'a',label:'Alpha',value:40},
  {id:'b',label:'Beta',value:25},
  {id:'c',label:'Gamma',value:20},
  {id:'d',label:'Delta',value:15},
]
<Treemap data={data} title="Market share" />

whenToUse: Showing part-to-whole proportions across many segments in a compact area
Visualising hierarchical magnitude where slice size encodes value
whenNotToUse: Few segments where a simple split reads better — use PieChart for ≤5
Precise value comparison — area encoding is approximate

related.name: PieChart
related.reason: Use for part-of-whole with five or fewer flat segments

related.name: Heatmap
related.reason: Use for magnitude across a two-dimensional grid

intent.a11yRationale: Renders with role="img" and requires a title prop for screen reader labeling.
